Description
= '''Level 901''', otherwise known as the "'''Destructured Lattice'''" or "'''VO-004'''," is an expansive network of vast, empty hallways suspended in a large abyss. Architecturally, the level consists of modern-esque carpeted halls, which float and rotate haphazardly around the space. They are completely devoid of any objects or decorations, other than a simple skylight and strewn about fluorescent lighting. The collective drone of hundreds of bulbs creates a quaky, low rumbling sound that can be heard reverberating across the level, albeit quite dampened and muted. The entire level seems to be blanketed in a fog which varies in thickness randomly. It seems to be a simple water vapor fog, similar to cloud formations, which show no anomalous or enigmatic properties. The level does have gravitational forces, but they are very weak, allowing one to float from platform to platform. This force has some variation in both direction and strength at times, although these are mostly inconsequential and barely noticeable. Two large elliptical “clouds” of matter and debris compose the main structural core of the level. The first is a large, slightly asymmetrical spheroid collection of halls measuring about 20km~ in every direction. The sub-section is tightly packed and very dense with matter, allowing easy traversal of the level for the first several kilometers or so. Once one leaves the boundaries of the first cloud, one might stumble upon the latter of the two clouds - a flatter disc-like expanse. Although this cloud is significantly sparser than the previous, even slowly fading away with distance, this particular cluster seems to be unbounded in size. Strangely, there is a very erratic pattern of halls materializing and dematerializing within the level. This typically happens in the central cloud of matter, however, is not exclusively limited to it alone. This almost always occurs out of sight, in areas secluded from any wanderers. These instances seem to have a slightly positive trend, which leads to the level sluggishly getting more compact bit by bit. It is debatable whether this mass of structures should be considered a level or not. The entirety of it is situated within the Void, and very scattered and dispersed. Most, if not all wanderers who arrive here are usually from either failed noclips or have been suspended in the Void for a long time. It is held together by an incredibly light, soft pull of gravity - enough to keep the individual parts from drifting but not enough to pull them all together at any perceptible speed. It simply seems to be a mere failed level, no different from a brown dwarf to a star. No entities preside at the level. There is no immediate danger within the level, other than the shifting of the level’s landscape, which can prove troublesome in rare occurrences, Other than that, the level is quite empty, so one should attempt to exit before running out of supplies. ---- =
Entrances & Exits
= All immediate entrances and exits to and from the level are based in [[The Void]]. Most wanderers arrive here after falling for extended periods of time, and most will leave the same way. Although some suspect entrances to [[The Whole|other voids]], void-like planes, or even other void objects are scattered and stowed away within the level. ---- [[File:Gguggug.jpg|center|frameless|500x500px]] '''Author:''' [[User:Potatoeshahaha|Potatoeshahaha]] {{Redirect|Prev=900|Next=902}} [[Category:Levels]] [[zh:Level 901]]
